title: Grade 5 Math
description: STAAR Grade 5 Math curriculum aligned to the Texas Essential Knowledge and Skills (TEKS). Topics build from decimal place value through volume, data, and personal financial literacy.

units.title: Place Value with Decimals
units.summary: Read, write, compare, and round decimals to the thousandths.

lessons.title: Decimal place value to thousandths
lessons.objective: Represent the value of a digit in decimals through thousandths.

questions.prompt: What is the value of the 7 in 4.187?
questions.explanation: The 7 is in the third position after the decimal point, the thousandths place.

lessons.title: Comparing & ordering decimals
lessons.objective: Compare and order two decimals to the thousandths.

questions.prompt: Which list is in order from greatest to least?

lessons.title: Rounding decimals
lessons.objective: Round decimals to a given place.

questions.prompt: Round 6.357 to the nearest hundredth.
questions.explanation: The digit after the hundredths place is 7, so round 6.35 up to 6.36.

units.title: Adding & Subtracting Decimals
units.summary: Add and subtract decimals using the standard algorithm.

lessons.title: Adding decimals
lessons.objective: Add decimals to the thousandths.

questions.prompt: What is 4.27 + 3.6?

lessons.title: Subtracting decimals
lessons.objective: Subtract decimals to the thousandths.

questions.prompt: What is 9.5 − 2.83?

units.title: Multiplying & Dividing Decimals
units.summary: Multiply and divide decimals by whole numbers and other decimals.

lessons.title: Multiplying decimals by whole numbers
lessons.objective: Multiply a decimal by a whole number.

questions.prompt: What is 0.6 × 7?

lessons.title: Multiplying decimal × decimal
lessons.objective: Multiply a decimal by a decimal to the hundredths.

questions.prompt: What is 0.4 × 0.3?

lessons.title: Dividing decimals by whole numbers
lessons.objective: Divide decimals by whole numbers.

questions.prompt: What is 5.6 ÷ 4?

units.title: Adding & Subtracting Fractions (Unlike Denominators)
units.summary: Add and subtract fractions and mixed numbers with unlike denominators.

lessons.title: Adding unlike fractions
lessons.objective: Add fractions with unlike denominators by finding a common denominator.

questions.prompt: What is 1/3 + 1/4?

lessons.title: Subtracting unlike fractions
lessons.objective: Subtract fractions with unlike denominators.

questions.prompt: What is 5/6 − 1/2?

units.title: Multiplying & Dividing Fractions
units.summary: Multiply a whole number by a fraction and divide unit fractions by whole numbers (and the reverse).

lessons.title: Whole number × fraction
lessons.objective: Represent and solve multiplication of a whole number by a fraction.

questions.prompt: What is 6 × 2/3?

lessons.title: Unit fraction ÷ whole number
lessons.objective: Divide a unit fraction by a whole number.

questions.prompt: What is 1/4 ÷ 2?
questions.explanation: Splitting 1/4 into 2 equal parts gives 1/8 each.

lessons.title: Whole number ÷ unit fraction
lessons.objective: Divide a whole number by a unit fraction.

questions.prompt: How many 1/3-cup servings are in 4 cups?

units.title: Order of Operations & Patterns
units.summary: Use order of operations and identify rules in numerical patterns.

lessons.title: Order of operations
lessons.objective: Simplify expressions using order of operations (no exponents).

questions.prompt: Simplify: 12 + 4 × (6 − 2)

lessons.title: Numerical patterns
lessons.objective: Generate a numerical pattern given a rule.

questions.prompt: If the rule is 'multiply by 2 and add 3', what comes after 5?

units.title: Coordinate Plane
units.summary: Plot ordered pairs in the first quadrant of the coordinate plane.

lessons.title: Plotting ordered pairs
lessons.objective: Identify and plot points using ordered pairs.

questions.prompt: Which ordered pair is 4 units right and 2 units up from the origin?
questions.explanation: Ordered pairs are (x, y), so 4 right then 2 up is (4, 2).

units.title: Classifying 2D Figures
units.summary: Classify two-dimensional figures into categories based on hierarchical attributes.

lessons.title: Quadrilateral hierarchy
lessons.objective: Classify quadrilaterals based on attributes.

questions.prompt: Which statement is ALWAYS true?
questions.explanation: A square is a special rectangle (4 right angles, opposite sides parallel) with all sides equal.

units.title: Volume of Rectangular Prisms
units.summary: Find volume of rectangular prisms using V = l × w × h.

lessons.title: Volume formula
lessons.objective: Apply V = l × w × h to find volume.

questions.prompt: A box has length 5 cm, width 3 cm, and height 4 cm. What is its volume in cubic cm?

lessons.title: Counting unit cubes
lessons.objective: Find volume by counting unit cubes.

questions.prompt: A prism is filled with 2 layers of unit cubes. Each layer is 4 by 3. What is the total volume?
questions.explanation: Each layer has 4 × 3 = 12 cubes, and there are 2 layers: 12 × 2 = 24.

units.title: Measurement Conversions
units.summary: Solve problems by converting within customary or metric measurement systems.

lessons.title: Customary conversions
lessons.objective: Convert customary units of length, weight, and capacity.

questions.prompt: How many ounces are in 3 pounds?

lessons.title: Metric conversions
lessons.objective: Convert metric units of length and capacity.

questions.prompt: How many milliliters are in 2.5 liters?

units.title: Data Analysis
units.reportingCategory: Data Analysis and Personal Financial Literacy
units.summary: Represent and interpret data with bar graphs, dot plots, scatterplots, and stem-and-leaf plots.

lessons.title: Reading scatterplots
lessons.objective: Interpret discrete data presented in a scatterplot.

questions.prompt: A scatterplot shows hours studied (x) vs. test score (y). Most points go up to the right. This pattern suggests…
choices: More study time tends to mean lower scores.
More study time tends to mean higher scores.
questions.answer: More study time tends to mean higher scores.
questions.explanation: An upward pattern (positive trend) means y increases as x increases.

units.title: Personal Financial Literacy
units.reportingCategory: Data Analysis and Personal Financial Literacy
units.summary: Identify income types, taxes, balance a simple budget, and weigh the cost of borrowing.

lessons.title: Gross vs net income
lessons.objective: Define gross income and net income.

questions.prompt: Lily earns $400 a week. After taxes, she takes home $340. The $340 is her…
questions.explanation: Net income is what remains after taxes are subtracted from gross income.

lessons.title: Simple budget
lessons.objective: Balance a simple budget by comparing income and expenses.

questions.prompt: Income is $250 and expenses are $215. How many dollars are left to save?
